methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $130M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Infrastructure & Logistics (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Infrastructure & Logistics, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Amperon
private.rationale: Same sector (Infrastructure & Logistics), same stage (Series B)

data.narrative:
Earth AI, a Series B infrastructure and logistics company, has raised $29M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a limited comparables universe: two private peers in the sector (AiDash and Amperon) with no public comps available, reflecting the early-stage maturity of this domain.

Given the scarcity of same-stage and same-tier peers, the peer-median approach was not applicable. Instead, a stage-progression method was employed, using the company's $130M implied valuation (derived from total capital raised) as a baseline. Applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B to subsequent-stage transitions yields an implied valuation of $247M. This represents the sole quantitative method applicable to the dataset.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $210M (low) to $284M (high), with a median point of $247M. The wide range reflects meaningful uncertainty: the single-method triangulation provides limited triangulation depth, and the sparse peer set offers minimal calibration against market comparables. Key risks to valuation include the strength of Earth AI's unit economics relative to the two identified peers, customer concentration, and the nascent state of the broader sector. Investors should treat this range as directional rather than definitive pending access to deeper peer financials or market transaction data.
